stories.headline: Impeachment prosecutors seek to open sealed box of Sara Duterte's tax records
stories.summary: House prosecutors asked the Senate impeachment court to open a sealed box of Bureau of Internal Revenue records on Vice President Sara Duterte and her husband during the pretrial stage, saying they want to inventory its contents rather than have them admitted as evidence at this point. The records were produced by the BIR in April and later kept sealed by the House justice committee on privacy grounds. Senate Secretary Renato Bantug Jr. said only the impeachment court can authorize opening the box. The pretrial conference was extended through Friday.
stories.context: Duterte was impeached by the House of Representatives over allegations that include misuse of confidential funds and unexplained wealth. The Senate is sitting as an impeachment court and has been marking thousands of pages of documentary evidence in pretrial conferences.
stories.watch_for: The impeachment trial is scheduled to open on July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: Philippine stock index drops below 6,000 as growth forecasts are cut
stories.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange index fell 2.2 percent, or 134.85 points, to close at 5,991.37 on June 24, its biggest single-day decline in more than three months, as investors reacted to lowered economic growth forecasts and a weaker peso. The peso closed at 61.552 to the US dollar, down 20.1 centavos. Economic Planning Secretary Arsenio Balisacan said this week the economy may grow 3.5 to 4.5 percent this year, below the government's 5 to 6 percent target, while S&P Global Ratings cut its forecast to 4.1 percent from 5.8 percent.
stories.context: Analysts have linked the weaker outlook to elevated oil prices stemming from the conflict between the United States and Iran, along with government underspending.

stories.headline: Francisco set to exit Philippine area; only Batanes under storm signal
stories.summary: PAGASA said Severe Tropical Storm Francisco, known internationally as Mekkhala, was accelerating north-northeastward and was expected to leave the Philippine Area of Responsibility on the afternoon or evening of June 25. As of the 5 a.m. bulletin it was about 415 kilometers northeast of Itbayat, Batanes, the only area still under Wind Signal No. 1. The state weather bureau said the southwest monsoon and the storm's outer winds could still bring strong to gale-force gusts over much of Luzon, Panay Island, Negros Occidental and Northern Samar.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said Tropical Storm Higos could enter the area on Thursday evening, when it would be given the local name Gardo, but is expected to stay far from land and exit by Friday.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano logs minor explosion as Alert Level 3 stays in place
stories.summary: The Office of Civil Defense said Mayon Volcano in Albay produced a minor explosion at about 6:58 p.m. on June 25, following two lava flows recorded the previous evening. Authorities said the volcano has been in continuous effusive eruption for 170 days. Alert Level 3, indicating relatively high unrest, remains in effect, and entry into the six-kilometer permanent danger zone around the volcano is prohibited.

stories.headline: Officials divided over lowering age of criminal responsibility after school attacks
stories.summary: The Juvenile Justice and Welfare Council opposed proposals to lower the minimum age of criminal responsibility, currently 15, with Executive Director Tricia Clare Oco saying harsher penalties do not reduce youth violence and that underlying causes should be addressed instead. The council said cases of children in conflict with the law fell 77 percent from 2016 to 2025. The Palace earlier said President Marcos is open to lowering the age, and the Philippine National Police proposed setting it at 12, following a June 22 school shooting in Tacloban City.
stories.context: Under the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act, children 15 and younger are exempt from criminal liability and are referred to social welfare agencies instead of being jailed.
stories.watch_for: A Senate committee hearing on the law is scheduled for July 1, 2026.

stories.headline: Authorities say they foiled another school attack threat in Leyte
stories.summary: Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said authorities took a 14-year-old student into custody in Leyte after she allegedly used multiple Facebook accounts to post a threat of an attack at Tolosa National High School. Remulla said Senator Bam Aquino relayed a tip about the online chatter and that the student was in custody by late June 24. He said the minor had no access to firearms and that her motive appeared to stem from personal and family issues. Because she is under 15, she was turned over to the Department of Social Welfare and Development.
stories.context: The case follows a June 22 shooting at a high school in Tacloban City that killed three students, and a separate stabbing of students in Negros Occidental.

stories.headline: Nueva Vizcaya court dismisses case against Bayombong bishop and anti-mining advocates
stories.summary: A court in Nueva Vizcaya dismissed charges against Bayombong Bishop Jose Elmer Mangalinao and other environmental advocates who had opposed a mining exploration project in the province, the bishop said in a statement on June 24. The complaint had been filed by a man who identified himself as a leader of the Bugkalot and Ilongot indigenous community over an anti-mining barricade set up in ancestral domain areas. Mangalinao described the ruling as a victory for environmental defenders.

stories.headline: Brownlee joins Gilas Pilipinas for FIBA World Cup qualifying road games
stories.summary: Naturalized player Justin Brownlee said he is ready to suit up for Gilas Pilipinas in upcoming FIBA World Cup Asian Qualifiers away games against New Zealand and Australia, days after helping Barangay Ginebra win the PBA Commissioner's Cup. The 38-year-old, a four-time PBA Best Import, scored a career-high 54 points in one game of the seven-game finals series against TNT.

stories.headline: Twin earthquakes kill at least 164 in Venezuela; state of emergency declared
stories.summary: Two earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck western Venezuela on the evening of June 24, killing at least 164 people and injuring about 971, acting President Delcy Rodriguez said. The government declared a state of emergency as rescuers searched collapsed buildings, with the worst damage reported in the capital, Caracas. Authorities closed the city's main airport and suspended classes. Officials said the death toll could rise as search-and-rescue operations continue.
stories.context: The US Geological Survey warned that high casualties were probable because many buildings in the affected area are made of unreinforced masonry and adobe, which are vulnerable to strong quakes.

stories.headline: Record temperatures hit France, UK and Spain in western Europe heatwave
stories.summary: A heatwave across western Europe pushed temperatures to record highs, with France recording its hottest day since national records began in 1947 and nearly 41 degrees Celsius reported in Paris. The UK logged its hottest June day on record at 36.1 degrees in Gosport, while Spain reported its highest June daily average since 1950. Red heat alerts covered much of France, and the Louvre and the Eiffel Tower closed early. French officials said at least 40 people have drowned in heat-related incidents since Thursday.
stories.watch_for: Forecasters said some relief is expected from Friday as temperatures gradually drop.

stories.headline: Oil prices fall to pre-war levels as Strait of Hormuz traffic resumes
stories.summary: Brent crude briefly fell below 72.48 US dollars a barrel, returning to levels last seen before the United States and Israel struck Iran in late February, as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz gradually resumed. Prices have eased since the US and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding on June 17. Maritime firms said about 80 vessels have crossed the strait since Monday, still below pre-war levels. US President Donald Trump ordered an investigation into oil companies he accused of keeping pump prices high.
stories.context: Iran had effectively closed the strait, a key route for oil and gas shipments, after the US and Israeli strikes earlier this year.
stories.watch_for: The June 17 memorandum set a 60-day period for negotiations on Iran's nuclear program and steps to end the war.
